# Carbon Mandi

> India's first hemp & napier biomass based green hydrogen initiative — turning farms into fuel for India's energy independence. "Farm to Fuel. Future for All."

Carbon Mandi is an IIT Ropar–incubated venture building a decentralized, biomass-based green hydrogen ecosystem. It uses fast-growing hemp and napier grass grown by farmers as feedstock to produce green hydrogen, lowering production cost, strengthening energy security, supporting India's Climate Goals 2030, and creating new rural income streams. The model spans a circular economy (green hydrogen, biochar, carbon credits, fiber, construction materials) and aligns with the National Green Hydrogen Mission and Atmanirbhar Bharat.

## Pages

- [Home](https://www.carbonmandi.com/): Overview of the green hydrogen mission, how it's made, who benefits, and the national opportunity.
- [Blog](https://www.carbonmandi.com/blogs/blog): Articles on green hydrogen, biomass, the circular economy, and India's clean-energy transition.
- [Projects](https://www.carbonmandi.com/projects): The national green hydrogen project and decentralized production plants.
- [Team](https://www.carbonmandi.com/team): The people behind Carbon Mandi.
- [Right to Climate](https://www.carbonmandi.com/right-to-climate): Climate goals, carbon-neutral initiatives, and tree planting.
- [Mandi](https://www.carbonmandi.com/mandi): The farm-to-fuel marketplace model.
- [Contact](https://www.carbonmandi.com/contact): Partnership, investment, research, and media enquiries.
